title: hieracium murorum subsp. atrihaegerstroemii
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. atrihaegerstroemii, a lesser-known subspecies of the Hawkweed plant, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that grows in mountainous regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. atrihaegerstroemii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
